description:
You are a security engineer who has just finished rotating AWS credentials for a small internal utility.  
To wrap-up the rotation you must prepare a tiny, isolated Python environment and produce two short files that the compliance bot will later collect.  Follow the specifications below *exactly*; the automated grader will check every byte.

1. Create a Python virtual environment at  
   /home/user/cred_rotator_env  

   • Use the built-in venv module (no third-party helpers).  
   • When finished, the directory /home/user/cred_rotator_env **must contain** its own “pyvenv.cfg” file and the usual “bin/” sub-directory.

2. Inside that virtual environment install exactly one package with pip:  
   python-dotenv version 1.0.0 (pin the version explicitly with “==”).  
   No other packages should appear in the final requirements list except the ones that come from pip’s own bootstrap.  

3. Freeze the third-party dependency list (just the direct, user-installed ones) into  
   /home/user/cred_rotator_env/requirements.txt  

   The file must contain exactly one line (plus the trailing newline):  
   python-dotenv==1.0.0

4. Store the freshly rotated credentials in a dotenv-style file located at  
   /home/user/rotated_credentials.env  

   The file’s **entire** content must be three lines, each ending with a UNIX newline character “\n” and with no leading/trailing spaces:  
   AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=NEWACCESSKEY123456  
   AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=newSecretKey890+/=  
   AWS_SESSION_TOKEN=FwoGZXEXAMPLESESSIONTOKEN\n

5. Write a single-line log entry confirming a successful rotation to  
   /home/user/rotation_success.log  

   The line must be *exactly* (including brackets, timestamp, spacing and newline):  
   [rotation] 2024-05-01 09:00:00 Credentials rotated and environment prepared\n

When everything is correct you will have:

• A functioning virtual environment at /home/user/cred_rotator_env  
• An installed package “python-dotenv” at version 1.0.0 registered inside that venv  
• requirements.txt with only “python-dotenv==1.0.0”  
• The dotenv credentials file with the three key/value pairs exactly as shown  
• A rotation_success.log file with the precise one-line entry above

Do not include any extra output or files—compliance is very strict.
truth:
Expected artefacts for verification:

1. Directory: /home/user/cred_rotator_env  
   • Must exist and contain file “pyvenv.cfg”  
   • The executable /home/user/cred_rotator_env/bin/pip must succeed with “pip show python-dotenv” returning Version: 1.0.0

2. File: /home/user/cred_rotator_env/requirements.txt  
   • Exact content (with trailing newline):  
     python-dotenv==1.0.0\n

3. File: /home/user/rotated_credentials.env  
   • Exact content (with trailing newline after each line):  
     AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=NEWACCESSKEY123456\n  
     AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=newSecretKey890+/=\n  
     AWS_SESSION_TOKEN=FwoGZXEXAMPLESESSIONTOKEN\n

4. File: /home/user/rotation_success.log  
   • Exact content (with trailing newline):  
     [rotation] 2024-05-01 09:00:00 Credentials rotated and environment prepared\n
name: task_000000_3d7a5b47
